channel.title: Troubleshooting on JavaOps
channel.description: Recent content in Troubleshooting on JavaOps

item.title: How to Recover Kubernetes Postgres Data After Accidentally Deleting a Deployment and PVC
item.description:
A real-world survival guide with best practices for PersistentVolume and PersistentVolumeClaim management in Kubernetes.

Table of Contents

  1. The Scenario — What Went Wrong
  2. Understanding PV, PVC and the Lifecycle
  3. Best Practices When Declaring PV and PVC
  4. Setting Up — Full Working Manifests
  5. Creating a Database in PostgreSQL
  6. Simulating the Accident — Deleting Deployment and PVC
  7. Recovering the Data
  8. Verifying the Data is Back
  9. Summary Cheat Sheet

1. The Scenario — What Went Wrong

You have a PostgreSQL pod running in Kubernetes, backed by a PersistentVolume. One day you (or a teammate) accidentally run:

item.title: Kubernetes ImagePullBackOff: Fixing Local Docker Image Issues in Minikube
item.description: Learn why Kubernetes fails to pull local Docker images and how to fix ImagePullBackOff errors in Minikube.

item.title: Fixing Disk I/O Bottlenecks and Latency on Linux
item.description: Learn how to identify and resolve disk I/O bottlenecks on Linux systems using practical tools and real-world troubleshooting techniques.

item.title: Troubleshooting SSH Timeouts, Slow Login, and Authentication Failures
item.description: A practical guide to diagnosing and fixing common SSH connection issues on Linux servers, including timeouts, slow logins, and authentication errors.
